justification: All 4 business_impact sub-fields (financial, operational, user, risk) are populated with substantive, multi-sentence analysis. stakeholders_affected lists 7 distinct groups (end customers, B2B/enterprise clients, customer support, operations/dispatch, product/analytics, API consumers, sales/account management) each with specific impact descriptions. Multiple escalations for missing information are present in the Recommendation section (ETA accuracy rate, service tier scope, API migration plan, B2B contract exposure). Contractual SLA risk is explicitly identified in both the risk field and stakeholders section. confidence.level is 78, well within the <=80 threshold, with clearly listed assumptions. Competitive positioning is addressed in both the financial impact ('logistics industry trend is toward MORE precision') and risk field ('Competitive risk — industry is moving toward tighter windows').

observations.text: Exceptionally thorough stakeholder analysis with 7 distinct groups, far exceeding the minimum 3 required
Strong escalation discipline — 5 explicit questions that cannot be answered without more context, including the critical contract review
Correctly identifies that OTD metric inflation creates a 'dangerous blind spot' — this shows genuine domain reasoning, not surface-level analysis
Proposes the domain-correct alternative (time ranges instead of date-only) which demonstrates Bird thinking beyond just assessment into constructive guidance
Identifies the pivot point: 'if current time-specific ETAs are frequently wrong, date-only may actually IMPROVE trust' — shows calibrated reasoning rather than one-sided analysis
Catches API breaking change risk and regulatory risk (perishables, medical supplies) which are non-obvious dimensions
WISMPO cost estimate ($3-8 per contact) adds concrete financial grounding

confidence.level of 78 is reasonable but could arguably be lower given the number of unknowns listed — the analysis explicitly states it cannot assess the 'single most important data point' (current ETA accuracy rate)

agent_output_excerpt:
# Domain Analysis — Delivery ETA Display Change

## Domain Analysis

```yaml
domain_analysis:
  business_context: >
    Delivery ETA communication is a core touchpoint in the last-mile logistics experience.
    The proposal changes the granularity of the delivery promise from exact timestamps
    (e.g., "Delivery by 14:30 on Friday") to date-only windows (e.g., "Delivery by Friday").
    This affects every customer-facing channel where ETAs are displayed: tracking pages,
    notification emails/
